description.en: Authorize.Net is a US payment gateway service provider that helps merchants accept electronic payments online, in person, and by phone, including card payments and eCheck/ACH, with tools such as hosted checkout and a virtual terminal.

excerpt: Payment gateway service provider for accepting card and bank payments online, in person, and by phone.

content_features.title: In-person and phone payments
content_features.description: Support card-present and mail/telephone-style acceptance using tools like virtual terminal and compatible readers (as offered).

content_features.title: Recurring and stored billing tools
content_features.description: Build subscription or installment-style billing flows using customer profiles and recurring payment features where applicable.

content_features.title: Security and risk tooling
content_features.description: Use gateway-side controls and monitoring to help reduce fraud attempts and protect payment data during checkout.

content_features.title: Disputes and transaction management
content_features.description: Search, reconcile, void, refund, and manage transaction lifecycle actions in a single merchant interface.

content_features.title: Multiple integration options
content_features.description: Choose between API-based integration, hosted checkout pages, or manual entry workflows depending on your technical needs.

tip: When you see higher declines, review the exact response and reason codes, then map them to targeted fixes such as AVS, CVV, fraud settings, or issuer-specific handling.

faq.question: Is Authorize.Net a payment gateway or a full payment processor?
faq.answer: Authorize.Net is primarily a payment gateway; you typically also need a merchant account/acquiring arrangement to process card payments.

faq.question: How can I reduce card declines and improve approval rates?
faq.answer: Start by logging issuer response and reason codes, then address common causes like AVS/CVV mismatches, inconsistent billing address formatting, and retry logic for soft declines.

faq.question: Can I take payments over the phone or manually key in a card?
faq.answer: Yes—Authorize.Net offers merchant tools such as a virtual terminal-style workflow for manually entering payments, depending on your account configuration.

faq.question: Does Authorize.Net support recurring billing?
faq.answer: Authorize.Net provides features commonly used for recurring billing and stored customer payment profiles; exact functionality depends on your integration and account setup.

faq.question: Where do I handle refunds, voids, and transaction lookups?
faq.answer: These are typically managed through the Authorize.Net merchant interface and/or via API, depending on how you integrate.

seo.seo_description: Authorize.Net is a payment gateway service provider supporting online, in-person, and phone payments, including card payments and eCheck/ACH.
seo.keywords: Authorize.Net,payment gateway,credit card processing,eCheck,ACH,virtual terminal,recurring billing
